name: housing_services_cold_chain_excursion_oakline_car_fair
premise: A Housing Services response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving housing services cold chain excursion oakline car fair. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before markets open. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as care vs fairness under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Omar Okoye
agents.identity: I am Omar Okoye, accountable for a defensible call in Housing Services when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays fairness for care.

agents.name: Sana Dubois
agents.identity: I am Sana Dubois, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Housing Services.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so fairness isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of care.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect fairness.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to fairness to secure care under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before markets open. Evidence is partial. Omar Okoye must decide under care vs fairness. What does Omar Okoye do?
